touches.ai_summary: Outbound LinkedIn message to Quinn Walsh introducing our call recording and coaching platform. Mentioned seeing PrismExchange's growth and suggested a brief conversation about how similar SaaS companies improve CS team performance.
next_steps: Quinn responded expressing interest in learning more

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Quinn with case study from similar-sized SaaS company. Proposed 15-minute call to discuss their current call recording setup and coaching workflows.

questions_asked.question: Do you integrate with our existing tech stack?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with most major CRMs and communication platforms. Happy to review your specific stack on a call.

next_steps: Quinn agreed to qualification call next week

touches.ai_summary: Brief qualification call with Quinn to understand current state. PrismExchange currently uses Fireflies but Quinn mentioned limitations around coaching workflows and manager visibility. Discussed team size (27 CS reps) and primary pain points.

questions_asked.question: What's working well with Fireflies today?
questions_asked.answer: Transcription is solid, but we lack structured coaching tools and it's hard for managers to spot trends across calls.

questions_asked.question: How does your coaching feature differ from basic transcription?
questions_asked.answer: We provide automated scorecards, trend analysis, and manager dashboards specifically built for CS teams, not just sales.

next_steps: Quinn to share current Fireflies contract details

touches.ai_summary: Second qualification call to dive deeper into CS team structure. Quinn explained they have 27 account managers covering different customer segments, and coaching is inconsistent. Managers spend hours manually reviewing calls.

questions_asked.question: How many managers oversee these 27 reps?
questions_asked.answer: Four CS managers, each handling 6-7 reps. They're stretched thin.

questions_asked.question: Can we try this before committing?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we offer a pilot program. Let's discuss scope on our next call.

next_steps: Quinn to loop in SVP Derek Wagner for discovery

touches.ai_summary: Quick sync with Quinn to confirm discovery call attendees and agenda. Confirmed Derek Wagner (SVP Revenue) will join the first discovery call. Quinn mentioned budget approval process requires Derek's sign-off.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled with Quinn and Derek

touches.ai_summary: First discovery call with Quinn and Derek. Discussed PrismExchange's growth trajectory and need to scale CS coaching without adding headcount. Derek emphasized ROI expectations and asked about implementation timeline. Quinn walked through current Fireflies usage and gaps.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the need to improve coaching now?
questions_asked.answer: We're scaling fast and our NRR is slipping. We need consistent coaching to retain customers as we grow.

questions_asked.question: What kind of ROI have other CS teams seen?
questions_asked.answer: Typical customers see 15-20% improvement in CSAT and 10-15% increase in upsell identification within 6 months.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation take?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 2-3 weeks for initial setup, then we run a 30-day pilot with a subset of your team.

next_steps: Quinn to gather current CSAT and coaching metrics

touches.ai_summary: Second discovery call focused on use cases. Quinn brought Peyton Morris (Account Manager) to discuss day-to-day workflows. Peyton described challenges with call prep and finding relevant past conversations. Discussed automation capabilities.

questions_asked.question: How do your reps currently prepare for customer calls?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly manual. They dig through Fireflies transcripts or listen to recordings. Very time-consuming.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform automatically surface relevant past conversations?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we use AI to tag and categorize calls by topic, customer issue, and outcome. Reps can search instantly.

next_steps: Quinn to confirm seat count and contract timeline

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Quinn and Peyton. Walked through platform setup, integrations with their current stack (Salesforce, Zoom, Slack), and coaching dashboard. Peyton tested search functionality and coaching scorecard builder. Quinn asked detailed questions about data security and compliance.

questions_asked.question: Where is call data stored and how is it encrypted?
questions_asked.answer: All data stored in SOC 2 compliant AWS infrastructure with encryption at rest and in transit. We can provide detailed security documentation.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize coaching scorecards for different CS segments?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely. You can create multiple scorecards and apply them to different teams or call types.

questions_asked.question: How does this compare to your current Fireflies setup?
questions_asked.answer: Fireflies is great for basic transcription, but we need the structured coaching layer and automation you're showing us.

next_steps: Quinn to test with 3-5 team members
Schedule follow-up to review pilot results

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email checking in on pilot progress. Shared additional resources on CS-specific coaching best practices and customer success story from similar company. Asked if Quinn needed any support during pilot.
next_steps: Quinn confirmed pilot going well, will have feedback next week

touches.ai_summary: Extended technical evaluation and pilot debrief with Quinn, Peyton, and two CS managers. Overall feedback positive—team loved automation and coaching dashboards. Discussed pricing for 27 seats and unlimited automation tier. Quinn mentioned budget constraints and asked about discounting. Began exploring contract terms.

questions_asked.question: What's the price difference between your automation tiers?
questions_asked.answer: Standard tier includes basic automation, unlimited tier adds advanced workflows and custom integrations. For 27 seats, unlimited is $28,700 annually.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on pricing given we're moving from Fireflies?
questions_asked.answer: We can discuss volume discounts and transition credits. Let me put together a proposal.

questions_asked.question: When does your Fireflies contract expire?
questions_asked.answer: Two months. We'd like to transition before then if pricing works.

objections: Pricing higher than current Fireflies spend
next_steps: Send formal proposal with pricing options

touches.ai_summary: Sent formal proposal via email with three pricing tiers. Recommended unlimited automation tier at $28,700 for 27 seats. Included ROI calculator and implementation timeline. Mentioned competitive transition discount available.
next_steps: Quinn to review with Derek and finance team

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email asking if Quinn had questions about proposal. Shared case study highlighting CS team that switched from Fireflies and improved coaching coverage by 40%. Reiterated timeline and transition support.

questions_asked.question: Can you break down exactly what's included in unlimited automation?
questions_asked.answer: Sent detailed feature comparison doc showing automated call summaries, smart routing, custom workflows, and API access for integrations.

next_steps: Quinn confirmed meeting with Derek scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Brief proposal call with Quinn to answer clarification questions before exec meeting. Discussed contract length (annual), payment terms (annual upfront vs quarterly), and onboarding timeline. Quinn mentioned Derek will focus on ROI justification.

questions_asked.question: Do you offer quarterly payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can structure quarterly payments with a small processing fee. Happy to include that option.

next_steps: Send updated proposal with payment options

touches.ai_summary: First exec meeting with Derek Wagner and Quinn. Derek focused heavily on ROI metrics and asked for customer references in similar SaaS companies. Discussed how platform impacts NRR and CS efficiency. Derek appeared supportive but cautious about pricing relative to budget.

questions_asked.question: Can you provide references from CS teams that switched from Fireflies?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, I'll connect you with two customers who made that exact transition and saw strong results.

questions_asked.question: What's your decision timeline?
questions_asked.answer: We need to decide within 3 weeks to align with our Fireflies contract end. Budget is approved but we're evaluating final pricing.

objections: Need to justify premium over current Fireflies cost

touches.ai_summary: Second exec meeting with Derek—originally scheduled but rescheduled by Derek due to conflicting board meeting. Rescheduled for two days later to continue ROI discussion and finalize terms.

Proposal review call with Quinn after exec meetings. Quinn mentioned Derek is supportive but finance team pushing back on price point. Discussed potential discount to get within budget. Quinn asked what flexibility we have on the $28,700 proposal.

questions_asked.question: What's the lowest you can go on pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Let me discuss with my leadership. If we can commit to annual contract with upfront payment, I may have room to move.

questions_asked.question: What's your internal budget target?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally under $22k to align with finance approval thresholds. I know that's a significant discount.

objections: Budget constraints—need to hit sub-$22k target

touches.ai_summary: Third exec meeting with Derek to discuss revised pricing proposal. Presented $21,700 ACV with annual upfront payment and transition support included. Derek appreciated the flexibility but wanted to confirm implementation timeline wouldn't slip. Discussed success metrics for first 90 days.

questions_asked.question: If we commit to this price, can you guarantee the 2-3 week implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we'll prioritize your onboarding and assign a dedicated implementation manager. Timeline is firm.

questions_asked.question: Does this pricing work within your budget?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, this gets us within range. We'll need to finalize contract terms and get legal review.

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Quinn to review contract terms. Discussed data retention policies, cancellation terms, and auto-renewal clause. Quinn requested some modifications to termination notice period and asked about seat expansion pricing.

questions_asked.question: If we need to add seats mid-contract, what's the pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Additional seats are prorated at the same per-seat rate for the remainder of the contract term.

next_steps: Send to Quinn for legal review

touches.ai_summary: Exec meeting with Derek to finalize contract redlines. Derek's legal team requested minor changes to liability cap and data processing addendum. Discussed SLA guarantees and support tier. Derek confirmed he's ready to sign pending final legal approval.

questions_asked.question: What's your standard SLA for platform uptime?
questions_asked.answer: 99.9% uptime guarantee with credits for any downtime beyond that threshold.

questions_asked.question: Are we good to proceed with legal changes?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, these are standard requests. Our legal team is reviewing now and should approve by end of week.

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Quinn to confirm final contract version incorporated all requested changes. Reviewed payment schedule (annual upfront), implementation milestones, and success criteria. Quinn confirmed Derek approved and contract going to procurement for processing.

questions_asked.question: When can we expect signatures?
questions_asked.answer: Procurement takes 3-5 business days. Derek already approved, so just administrative processing now.

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing confirmation call with Quinn. Reviewed invoice details, payment terms (annual upfront payment of $21,700), and confirmed 27 seat licenses with unlimited automation tier. Discussed onboarding schedule starting next week.

questions_asked.question: When will we receive the final invoice?
questions_asked.answer: Invoice will be sent within 24 hours of signed contract. Payment due within 30 days, but onboarding starts immediately upon signature.
